data.body:
PASS-1 review (T5c Security overlay) — one finding + affirmations.

FINDING (T5c — data protection / encryption at rest): The Gerrit data EBS volume and the instance root volume declare no `encrypted = true`. `infra/terraform/main.tf`: `aws_ebs_volume.data` (gp3 at /var/gerrit) and `aws_instance.gerrit.root_block_device` set only `type`/`size`. This volume stores NoteDb — all git repos, change/review data, and `refs/meta/config`, which per ADR-0014 carries the bot HTTP token (a real in-band secret). EBS at-rest encryption is not addressed in ADR-0012, gerrit-aws-setup.md, or the plan's out-of-scope note (which scopes out only "KMS-CMK state encryption" for S3 tfstate — a different surface). Notably the S3 state bucket IS encrypted (SSE-S3, `infra/bootstrap/main.tf`), so the omission on EBS reads as an oversight, not a deliberate scoping decision. `encrypted = true` (AWS-managed key) is a one-line, no-cost addition.

- [ ] Set `encrypted = true` on `aws_ebs_volume.data` and on the instance `root_block_device` in `infra/terraform/main.tf` (AWS-managed SSE), OR record an explicit, justified at-rest exemption in ADR-0012 covering the token-bearing `refs/meta/config`.

AFFIRMATIONS (T5c passes):
- Trust-boundary auth is explicit on every reachable surface. Public ingress (443/80/29418, `network.tf`) is enumerated with rationale; the `/review/` public endpoint is token-gated (S4b) and the receiver is loopback-bound + not SG-open. Inbound webhook auth is a well-reasoned two-layer control (private compose network primary + URL token secondary) that is honest about the plugin's no-HMAC limitation (ADR-0014). No inbound port 22 — admin via SSM Session Manager.
- In transit: TLS terminated at nginx (Let's Encrypt) for the public surface; internal Gerrit->receiver hop is plain HTTP on the trusted private network with stated rationale.
- Least privilege: instance role scopes SSM read to `arn:...:parameter/rebar/prod/*` (no account wildcard) with `kms:Decrypt` bounded by a `kms:ViaService` condition; single-owner role contract; S2/S4a add no speculative grants. GitHub replication identity is a single-repo write-only deploy key.
- Secret lifecycle: secrets are SSM SecureString placeholders (no plaintext in IaC); `user_data.sh` fetches via IMDSv2, writes `/etc/rebar/.env` at 0600, fails fast on the CHANGEME sentinel, and does not echo secret values to logs; the bot token is rotatable via a single scripted path.
